sanskrit:
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते।
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः॥
transliteration:
aphalākāṅkṣibhir yajño vidhi-dṛṣṭo ya ijyate |
yaṣṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ ||
literal_meaning: A sacrifice performed by those free from desire for results, according to scriptural ordinance, with the mind fixed in the conviction 'it is to be done,' is of the nature of goodness.
contextual_meaning: True psychological health arises when action is divorced from outcome-dependent reward, aligning behavior with intrinsic values rather than extrinsic reinforcement.

shadow: Residual guilt over past transactional worship

psychological_state: Flow-state of value-consistent action; ego-transcendent discipline
trigger_scenario: Volunteering at a shelter without posting about it, simply because 'helping is what humans do.'
anchor_text: Do what is right because it is right, not for what it brings.
